overview.short: Kunjargad Fort or Kombad Fort is a fort located 135 km (84 mi) from Kalyan by way of Khireshwar.
overview.description: Kunjargad Fort or Kombad Fort is a fort located 135 km (84 mi) from Kalyan by way of Khireshwar. The fort is in Akole taluka in Ahmednagar district in Maharashtra. The name of the fort is derived from the word “kunj”, meaning elephant in Sanskrit, due to the fort's resemblance to an elephant.

topPlaces.name: Pimpalgaon Joge Dam
topPlaces.description: Pimpalgaon Joge Dam, is an earthfill dam on Ar Pushpavati river, a tributary of Kukadi River near Junnar, Pune district in the state of Maharashtra in India.

topPlaces.name: Harishchandragad
topPlaces.description: Harishchandragad is a hill fort in the Ahilyanagar district of India. Its history is linked with that of Malshej Ghat, Kothale village and it has played a role in guarding and controlling the surrounding region.

items.note: Pimpalgaon Joge Dam, is an earthfill dam on Ar Pushpavati river, a tributary of Kukadi River near Junnar, Pune district in the state of Maharashtra in India.

Harishchandragad is a hill fort in the Ahilyanagar district of India. Its history is linked with that of Malshej Ghat, Kothale village and it has played a role in guarding and controlling the surrounding region.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kunjargad?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kunjargad is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

The nearest airport is Shirdi Airport (63 km) and the nearest railway station is Nashik Road (62 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kunjargad?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kunjargad at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kunjargad?
faq.a: Stays in Kunjargad start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kunjargad?
faq.a: The top attractions are Pimpalgaon Joge Dam, Harishchandragad.
